name: designing-apis
description: Designs REST and GraphQL APIs including endpoints, error handling, versioning, and documentation. Use when creating new APIs, designing endpoints, reviewing API contracts, or when asked about REST, GraphQL, or API patterns.
capabilities: Design RESTful API endpoints using resource-based URL patterns
Define proper HTTP status codes and response formats
Implement API versioning via URL paths or headers
Create GraphQL schemas with queries, mutations, and types
Apply security and rate limiting best practices
author: CloudAI-X
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with API design guidance, templates, and best practices. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access beyond reading its own documentation files. All static findings are false positives: markdown code block delimiters were misidentified as shell backticks, example URLs flagged as hardcoded URLs are non-sensitive placeholders, JWT references flagged as weak cryptography are standard API authentication patterns, and HTTP method documentation was misidentified as system reconnaissance.
valueStatement: Creating APIs without clear design patterns leads to inconsistent endpoints and poor developer experience. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for designing REST and GraphQL APIs with standardized endpoints, proper error handling, versioning strategies, and OpenAPI documentation templates.
userTitle: Design REST and GraphQL APIs with best practices
limitations: Does not generate implementation code for API backends
Does not validate external API specifications
Does not integrate with API gateways or testing tools
Does not execute or deploy APIs

useCases.title: Design new API endpoints
useCases.description: Create consistent REST and GraphQL API designs following industry best practices

useCases.title: Plan API architecture
useCases.description: Define API contracts, versioning strategies, and documentation standards

useCases.title: Review API designs
useCases.description: Validate API contracts against established design patterns

promptTemplates.title: Basic REST design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a REST API for user management including list, create, get, update, and delete operations

promptTemplates.title: GraphQL schema
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a GraphQL schema for an e-commerce app with products, orders, and user types

promptTemplates.title: OpenAPI documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write an OpenAPI 3.0 specification for a blog API with posts, comments, and authors

promptTemplates.title: Error handling design
promptTemplates.prompt: Design a comprehensive error handling strategy for a REST API including status codes and error response formats

bestPractices: Use nouns, not verbs, in endpoint URLs for REST APIs
Apply consistent response envelopes across all endpoints
Include pagination and filtering parameters for list endpoints
Document all authentication requirements in OpenAPI specs
antiPatterns: Using verbs in URLs like /getUsers or /createPost
Returning different response formats for success vs error
Exposing sensitive data in URL parameters
Skipping versioning and breaking changes without notice

faq.question: Does this skill generate code?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides API design guidance, templates, and best practices. It does not generate implementation code.

faq.question: What API styles are supported?
faq.answer: REST and GraphQL patterns are covered with endpoints, schemas, mutations, and query patterns.

faq.question: Can it validate my existing API spec?
faq.answer: The skill provides validation checklists but does not execute against external specifications.

faq.question: Is my data safe?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-based skill that reads only its own documentation files. No data leaves your session.

faq.question: What OpenAPI version is supported?
faq.answer: OpenAPI 3.0.3 specification templates are provided with full component and schema examples.

faq.question: How is this different from code generation tools?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on API design and contract definition rather than writing backend or frontend code.
